title: abutilon subumbellatum
intro: Abutilon subumbellatum, also known as the Cape lantern, is a flowering plant species native to South Africa, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and green foliage. Traditionally, Abutilon subumbellatum has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The plant has been found to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Abutilon subumbellatum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat ailments such as fever, coughs, and skin irritations.
